title: [4.6.1-rhel] linux, rootless: clamp oom_score_adj if it is too low
body:
backport of:

- 19bd9b33dd8c8f166327f4e1ab18e0cd5afc9180
- 8b4a79a744ac3fd176ca4dc0e3ae40f75159e090

it solves a problem with docker-compose for rootless since it hard codes the oom-score-adj to 0.


```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *giuseppe* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/v4.6.1-rhel/OWNERS)~~ [giuseppe] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Do you have a bz/jira for this?

> Do you have a bz/jira for this?

there is no bz/jira as it is not a problem yet.  This is to prevent issues once we upgrade crun to 1.9, since old versions of crun ignore `oom_score_adj` set to 0.

AFAIK we are not allowed to backport things this late without approved bz or now Jira bug I guess.
cc @TomSweeneyRedHat
As @Luap99 we need a Jira card for this
ok so let's wait it gets into RHEL through the next release
